CheckTitle: Cosmos DB account enforces TLS 1.2 or higher
Description: **Azure Cosmos DB accounts** are evaluated for **minimum TLS version**. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated and contain known weaknesses. Enforcing **TLS 1.2** ensures all client connections negotiate modern, secure encryption protocols.
Risk: Allowing **TLS 1.0/1.1** exposes client connections to **POODLE**, **BEAST**, and other protocol downgrade attacks that can compromise the **confidentiality** and **integrity** of data in transit, and may enable credential interception via weakened cipher suites.

Code.Other:
1. Sign in to the Azure portal and open your Cosmos DB account
2. In the left menu, select Networking
3. Locate the Minimum TLS version setting
4. Select TLS 1.2
5. Click Save
6. Validate that all client applications support TLS 1.2 before enforcing the change
Code.Terraform:
```hcl
# Terraform: Enforce minimum TLS 1.2 on a Cosmos DB account
resource "azurerm_cosmosdb_account" "<example_resource_name>" {
  name                = "<example_resource_name>"
  resource_group_name = "<example_resource_name>"
  location            = "<example_location>"
  offer_type          = "Standard"
  kind                = "GlobalDocumentDB"

  geo_location {
    location          = "<example_location>"
    failover_priority = 0
  }

  minimal_tls_version = "Tls12" # Critical: Rejects client connections negotiating TLS 1.0 or 1.1
}
```

Recommendation.Text: Set the Cosmos DB account **minimum TLS version** to at least **1.2** to block legacy protocols (`TLS 1.0`/`1.1`) vulnerable to known downgrade and cipher attacks. Inventory and update **client SDKs** and **drivers** to support TLS 1.2 prior to enforcement, and pair this control with **private endpoints**, **AAD/RBAC authentication**, and **handshake failure monitoring** to identify outdated clients.
